0.title: Baseline, inventory, and safety rails
completedGates: price-history transport failure aborts instead of replacing valid history (update-prices.py, tested)
all R2-writing workflows share one concurrency group (r2-bucket-writers, cancel-in-progress: false)
Python dependencies pinned in .github/scripts/requirements.txt; workflows install from it
quality-gates.yml runs API tests (tests/api) and Python tests (.github/scripts/tests)
daily-pricing schedule moved 12:00 -> 14:00 UTC so it no longer races the online-meta build it reads

R2 inventory measured and checked in (.github/baselines/r2-inventory-2026-07-12.json): 112,267 objects, 4.27 GB of the 8 GB budget; steady-state ~294 writes/day; full player rebuild rewrites all 89k player bodies
shared Python R2 module (.github/scripts/lib/r2.py): adaptive-retry client + found/missing/corrupt/transport read results adopted by all 7 Python producers (12 new tests)

shared Node R2 module (.github/scripts/lib/r2.mjs): adaptive-retry client + typed read results adopted by all 6 Node producers; majors-trends and synonym-update no longer treat transport failures as missing data (11 new tests)
reproducible route-data benchmark (scripts/benchmark-routes.ts, npm run bench:routes) with mobile profile; baseline in .github/baselines/route-benchmark-2026-07-12.json
p50/p95 budgets set from measured baseline (see measuredBaselines.browser)
notes: Workflow sleeps in trends/player-aggregator intentionally retained until the build graph replaces them (plan: Phase 0 work list).
Shared concurrency group means GitHub may displace a pending writer run; accepted by plan until the Phase 5 reconciling workflow rediscovers dirty state from receipts.

1.title: Freeze semantics with contracts and golden fixtures
completedGates: normalized-layer contract shared/data/contracts.ts (schema v1): types, stable IDs, archetype identity triple, frozen SuccessTagPolicy, all-errors validator
canonical JSON serialization + sha256 content hashing (shared/data/{canonicalJson,hash}.ts)
golden fixtures for labs-event and online-window covering every plan-required edge case (tests/fixtures/data-pipeline/)
representability, invariant-rejection (30+ mutations), permutation-invariance, and ID-stability tests (tests/data/contracts.test.ts)

adversarial review pass: validator hardened (match arity/winner, participant records/flags, printing UID padding+name, back-reference reconciliation, policy recomputation, storage order, subtype consistency, constructor hygiene)
compatibility decisions D1-D11 recorded in semanticDifferences

serving-artifact contract shared/data/artifacts.ts: CardReport/CardUsageIndex/ConversionIndex/ArchetypeIndex schemas, all-errors validators with artifact-level invariants, and pure reference builders from normalized decks
golden snapshot hashes for all artifacts from both fixtures; permutation-invariance and rebuild byte-identity proven (20 tests)
notes: Exit criteria: representability proven via fixtures for both adapters; all 11 legacy inconsistencies have documented decisions; fixed inputs produce byte-identical normalized records AND serving artifacts.
Reference builders are the target for Phase 2 producer migration; archetype displayName is the lexicographically-smallest label (deterministic) pending the Phase 2 presentation merge.

2.title: Consolidate domain logic
completedGates: card identity + canonical-print policy consolidated into shared/data/{cardIdentity,canonicalPrint}.ts; old shared modules are thin re-exports; 12-scenario parity corpus pins the legacy canonical-print.mjs until its producer migrates (dcf3bd4)

success tags: every TS producer (functions/lib, .github/scripts/*.ts) computes from the one frozen SUCCESS_TAG_POLICY; grid parity test (placements 1-40 x 11 field sizes + edge inputs) pins run-online-meta.mjs until conversion (fc5e493)
frontend derivation copy in src/utils/clientSideFiltering.ts replaced by the shared policy (4c10c42)
card report builder consolidated into shared/data/reports/cardReport.ts with D9 deterministic tie-breakers; reportBuilder.ts is a shim; parity suite pins pre-move algorithm (457acdb)
legacy cardUsage + conversion builders consolidated into shared/data/reports/{cardUsage,conversion}.ts; byte-identical to the live .mjs producer and Python semantics, 7 parity tests (9902ba5)

archetype identity/presentation/build consolidated into shared/data/archetypes/{identity,presentation,build}.ts; stale Functions thumbnail engine retired (recorded improvement: snapshot archetypes can now infer thumbnails); Python icon behavior ported; 11 parity tests (afb21cb)

trends/matchup aggregation authority moved to shared/data/analysis/archetypeTrends.ts with explicit input contracts; survey confirmed no other TS aggregation copies exist (src/lib/matchups.ts is read-side presentation); behavior byte-preserved (1ae15b7)

normalized contract extended with Labs source fields (Participant points/icons/dropRound/labsDeckId/deckName; EventMeta country/city/eventType/updatedAt/completed/started/playersRound1/decklistCount/rk9Id/playlatamId/labsCode/sourceTournamentId), validated, opw scale documented as adapter-converted; content-hash snapshots unchanged (6aef355)
event match serving builders (shared/data/reports/eventMatches.ts: playerMatches + canonical matches) built from normalized records, 7 tests (e514600)
matchupProfiles quality-model builder (shared/data/reports/matchupProfiles.ts) ported from aggregate_matchups; groups by archetype key so casing variants collapse (D3); 8 tests (bb3bf17)

event artifact orchestrator (shared/data/reports/eventArtifacts.ts): one pure fn NormalizedEvent -> full serving tree (master/decks/players/matches/playerMatches/conversion/matchupProfiles/cardUsage/archetypes index+per-archetype files/index/meta + phase2/topcut slices), permutation-invariant, 9 tests (78c2665)

TS event build CLI (.github/scripts/event-cli.ts): validate normalized event -> build -> write local or upload R2; malformed record fails before publication; run end-to-end on the fixture (31 artifacts), 2 tests (0d5865d)

Labs source->normalized adapter (shared/data/adapters/labsSource.ts): applies ALL policy (IDs, canonicalization, archetype identity, success tags, outcome derivation) in TS; output validates + drives the orchestrator; 8 tests (78523c8)

event CLI accepts --from labs-source (adapts before validate/build); Python scraper emits the loose source shape (lib/labs_source.py) with a round-trip test driving the real TS adapter+CLI (1909db9, 706abf3)
download-tournament.py additively emits the source record under EMIT_SOURCE_EVENT (shadow, legacy path unchanged) (ff24ecb)
outstandingGates: wire a shadow build step (download-tournament.yml / a data-build workflow) that runs event-cli on the emitted source and compares against legacy artifacts (fixture/prod parity) - this is the Phase 6 cutover gate
event-cli reindex/backfill-conversion/rebuild-catalog subcommands so reprocess-event-indexes.py/backfill-conversion.py/rebuild-tournaments-index.py stop importing the Python monolith
memorial generation migrated to shared builders
run-online-meta.mjs -> TS orchestration: DEFERRED pending a verified fixture-diff port
DECISION: tournament.db + cardIndex.json have no consumers; retire in Phase 7
notes: The entire TS receiving pipeline is complete and proven end-to-end: LabsSourceEvent -> adapter -> validated NormalizedEvent -> orchestrator -> full serving tree, invoked via event-cli, with a Python->TS round-trip test. Remaining Phase 2 is workflow shadow wiring + aux-script re-points + memorial; the destructive removal of Python/legacy generators is the Phase 6/7 cutover.

3.title: Build graph and shadow receipts
completedGates: canonical JSON + sha256 content hashing (shared/data/{canonicalJson,hash}.ts, Phase 1)

content-hash build graph: computeNodeKey (contract+builder version + sorted dep hashes + logical window, never the repo commit), topoSort with cycle/unknown-dep detection, planBuild dirty-node planner (shared/data/build/graph.ts); tests prove synonym-only change dirties exactly declared descendants, no-change replan is empty, missing receipt invalidates (9449772)

immutable release manifest + browser path resolver: composeRelease (independently versioned scope roots, unchanged scopes reuse prior root), validateReleaseManifest (immutable /releases/v1 paths only), resolveScopePath/resolveEventPath (shared/data/build/release.ts) (7b5ef90)

publish/receipt algorithm over an abstract ObjectStore: create-only immutable writes, read-back length+hash verify, receipt written LAST; body/verify failure never writes a receipt; idempotent identical re-publish; differing-content-at-immutable-key rejected (shared/data/build/receiptStore.ts) (085a5de)

R2 ObjectStore adapter (.github/scripts/lib/build/r2ObjectStore.mjs): create-only If-None-Match:* immutable writes + one-year cache; VERIFIED LIVE against the ciphermaniac-reports bucket (second write conflicts, read-back matches, missing returns null) (51f824d)

build-loop engine (shared/data/build/buildLoop.ts): plan -> build dirty in topo order -> publish immutably -> receipt last -> replan to convergence; no-change rerun builds nothing/writes nothing; synonym-only change rebuilds only descendants; plan mode writes nothing (f260c51)

LIVE shadow publication verified end-to-end against the production R2 bucket (.github/scripts/shadow-build.ts): built the fixture event through the orchestrator, published 31 immutable releases/v1 objects + node receipt + shadow channel pointer, hash-verified all on read-back, and GC'd to zero — never touching reports/ or production.json (14f6944)
outstandingGates: wire all producer scopes as build-graph nodes (event pipeline done; online/trends/players/prices/catalogs/snapshots builders call their shared builders) and run a full-graph shadow build
ETag conditional channel-pointer write with replan-on-conflict (store.put is unconditional; production.json needs it)
protected shadow workflow (.github/workflows) running the loop on schedule without touching production.json
notes: Every load-bearing abstraction of the content-hash build graph is implemented and tested (in-memory + one live R2 verification of the immutable-write gate). What remains is wiring the concrete producer nodes and running a live shadow build - which per the plan's own gates (shadow publication, performance measurement, rollback testing, dual writes through 14+ releases) is a staged, measured rollout best done with human oversight rather than an unattended autonomous run against production.

exitCriteria.shadow manifests describe every producti: PARTIAL - one event shadow proven live; full multi-scope graph (online/trends/players/prices/catalogs/snapshots) wiring pending
exitCriteria.receipt and pointer updates use conditio: PARTIAL - immutable If-None-Match:* live-verified; ETag conditional channel-pointer write still TODO

6.title: Regenerate and cut over all data

browser.profile: mobile 390x844, 4x CPU throttle, slow-4G (1.6 Mbps / 150ms RTT)

budgets.rule: no added blocking/route-data requests; regressions capped at min(5%, 50ms) on p95 first-data per plan

semanticDifferences.legacy: decks.json playerId means tp_id (Python events) but global handle (online); consumers sample-detect
semanticDifferences.decision: contract separates participantId (event-scoped) from playerRef (global, nullable); both stored on decks

semanticDifferences.legacy: item pct/dist 0-100 but archetype index percent is a 0-1 fraction; client infers scale from magnitude
semanticDifferences.decision: one representation: 0-100 with Pct field suffix; normalized layer stores counts, serving layer computes Pct; legacy fraction files get migration fixtures

semanticDifferences.legacy: producers preserve archetype case in slugs; shared TS lowercases -> split identities
semanticDifferences.decision: archetype identity triple: key (lowercased comparison), displayName (cased), slug (derived from key, never from sanitized display labels)

semanticDifferences.legacy: online + Python read item set/number from first-seen variant meta; can disagree with canonical UID after synonym rewrite
semanticDifferences.decision: set/number always derived from canonical UID and validated against it (reportBuilder.ts behavior wins)

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python master.json / archetype cards.json not synonym-canonicalized; only derived indexes are
semanticDifferences.decision: all serving reports canonicalize through the one shared builder

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python canonicalize_variant lstrips zeros ('18A') vs normalize_card_number pads ('018A'); three variants incl. SQLite
semanticDifferences.decision: canonical padded form (018A) everywhere; index keys remain a derived stripped lookup helper

semanticDifferences.legacy: phase2/topcut success tags only on labs events; guard implementations differ
semanticDifferences.decision: one versioned SuccessTagPolicy; phase tags apply only where phases exist (explicit in policy, not producer-local code)

semanticDifferences.legacy: archetype index fields differ per producer (icons Python-only; reportGenerator has neither icons nor signatureCards)
semanticDifferences.decision: one presentation module (Phase 2) merges thumbnail/signature/icon behavior; index schema lists all three explicitly

semanticDifferences.legacy: report items sorted by found only; ties are input-order-dependent
semanticDifferences.decision: explicit total-order tie-breakers (found desc, then name, then uid) required by contract; permutation tests enforce

semanticDifferences.legacy: reportVersion 3.1 on events only; no schemaVersion anywhere; volatile fetchedAt used as fingerprint
semanticDifferences.decision: schemaVersion + builderVersion on all normalized partitions, artifacts, receipts, manifests; volatile fetch time excluded from semantic hashes

semanticDifferences.legacy: online windows carry no match data; raw Labs winner codes surface to consumers
semanticDifferences.decision: normalized Match with explicit outcome enum (win/loss/tie/double_loss/bye/unpaired/unknown); online windows have matches: [] explicitly; raw winner codes stay in source captures

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python canonicalize_variant strips number '0' to empty (card skipped); shared TS normalizeCardNumber pads to '000'
semanticDifferences.decision: shared TS helper behavior wins ('0' is not a real card number; unrealistic edge, no production data affected)

semanticDifferences.legacy: Labs opw/oopw stored raw (0-1 fraction) in players.json
semanticDifferences.decision: source adapter multiplies by 100; contract opwPct/oopwPct always 0-100

discoveredDependencyEdges: measured: player-aggregator full rebuilds rewrite every players/{id} body (89,177 objects observed rewritten in one week) — confirms Phase 5 content-hash fingerprint requirement
